question: What is the definition of the acronym DHRS?

nuscale_ch01_0043.title: Ultimate Heat Sink
nuscale_ch01_0043.summary: The Ultimate Heat Sink for the NuScale plant is a large, stainless steel-lined, reinforced concrete pool located below grade, comprising the reactor, refueling, and spent fuel pool areas. During normal operations, heat is removed via a cooling system and rejected to the atmosphere, while in the event of a total loss of AC power, decay heat is passively transferred to the pool, which has sufficient water inventory to ensure continued cooling through heat-up and boiling.

nuscale_ch01_0450.title: BTP 10-2, Rev 4: Design Guidelines for Avoiding Water Hammers in Steam GeneratorsTFSGD B.1 thru B.4Top-Feed Steam Generator Designs
nuscale_ch01_0450.summary: This section explains that the NuScale plant design does not use top-feed, preheat, or once-through steam generator designs, so the specific regulatory guidelines for avoiding water hammers in these systems are not applicable. Instead, NuScale uses a different system (DHRS) that operates via passive natural circulation and inherently avoids the water hammer issues addressed by these guidelines.

nuscale_ch01_0669.title: DSRS 15.1.1-15.1.4, Rev 0: Decrease in Feedwater Temperature, Increase in Feedwater Flow, Increase in Steam Flow, and Inadvertent Opening of the Turbine Bypass System or Inadvertent Operation of the Decay Heat Removal SystemII.4 Specific CriterionInstrument Spans and Setpoints use RG 1.105
nuscale_ch01_0669.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for DSRS 15.1.1-15.1.4, addressing events such as changes in feedwater temperature or flow, steam flow, and inadvertent system operations. It confirms that NuScale meets specific criteria for system pressure, critical heat flux, event classification, and instrument setpoints, aligning with NRC guidance and ensuring plant safety under these conditions.

nuscale_ch01_0673.title: DSRS 15.1.1-15.1.4, Rev 0: Decrease in Feedwater Temperature, Increase in Feedwater Flow, Increase in Steam Flow, and Inadvertent Opening of the Turbine Bypass System or Inadvertent Operation of the Decay Heat Removal SystemII.3 Analytical ParametersCore Burnup
nuscale_ch01_0673.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for DSRS 15.1.1-15.1.4, which address events such as decreases in feedwater temperature, increases in feedwater or steam flow, and inadvertent operation of the turbine bypass or decay heat removal systems. It confirms that analytical parameters—including initial power level, conservative scram, core burnup, and setpoint inaccuracies—are addressed and conform to regulatory guidance, with no exceptions noted.

nuscale_ch01_0014.title: Acronyms and Abbreviations
nuscale_ch01_0014.summary: This section explains the conventions used for numbering tables, figures, and pages in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR), as well as the handling of proprietary and sensitive information. It clarifies that sensitive content is withheld from the public version in accordance with regulatory requirements, while all such material is clearly marked in the non-public version.

nuscale_ch01_0013.title: Proprietary Information
nuscale_ch01_0013.summary: This section explains the organization and identification of text, tables, figures, and page numbering within the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR). It also clarifies that while the FSAR does not contain proprietary or safeguards information, some sensitive content is withheld from public disclosure in accordance with regulations, and a separate public version is provided with such material removed.

nuscale_ch01_0117.title: Figure 1.7-3f: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0117.summary: This section provides the legend for piping and instrumentation diagrams (P&IDs) used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It lists and describes various symbols representing sample probes, flow and pressure measurement devices, temperature and level sensors, and other auxiliary instrumentation, ensuring standardized interpretation of the diagrams.

nuscale_ch01_0111.title: Figure 1.7-2: Instrumentatio n and Controls Symbol Legend
nuscale_ch01_0111.summary: This section provides a legend explaining the symbols used for instrumentation and controls in NuScale’s safety analysis diagrams. It describes the logic gate functions, control room indicators, switch types, and other symbols relevant to system status and operations, ensuring clear interpretation of the diagrams. Additionally, it includes notes on piping and instrumentation diagram conventions, such as vent and drain representations.

edges.description: Section B explains that the NuScale DHRS serves a similar function to the Auxiliary Feedwater (AFW) system in large PWRs, while Section A describes the DHRS as a system for removing decay heat and cooling the RCS, thus both sections connect by clarifying the role and function of DHRS in the plant.

Section A describes the function and context of the DHRS (Decay Heat Removal System), while Section B references regulatory criteria and events involving the DHRS, connecting them through the system's operational role in safety analysis.

Section A describes the function and operation of the DHRS (Decay Heat Removal System), while Section B references scenarios involving the operation of the Decay Heat Removal System, thus Section A provides details/specifications for the general mention of DHRS in Section B.

Section A states that the NuScale DHRS fulfills a similar function as the AFW system in large PWRs, while Section B references the Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) in the context of specific accident scenarios, connecting both sections through the functional role of DHRS.

Section A states that the NuScale DHRS fulfills a similar function as the AFW system in large PWRs, while Section B references the Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) in the context of inadvertent operation, connecting both sections through the functional role of DHRS.

Section A provides general information about acronyms and abbreviations in the NuScale FSAR, while Section B uses the acronym 'Decay Heat Removal System' (DHRS) in the context of specific DSRS events, connecting them by the use and context of the acronym in regulatory analysis.

Section A explains that the FSAR uses the DSRS and SRP as technical guidance for the NuScale design, while Section B provides specific DSRS criteria related to system operations including the Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS), thus Section A establishes the regulatory framework that Section B details with specific requirements.

Section B explains that the NuScale DHRS (Decay Heat Removal System) serves a similar function to the Auxiliary Feedwater (AFW) system in large PWRs, thereby providing context for the acronym DHRS, which is not defined in Section A.
